error.signature: AI recommends houseplants or flowers toxic to cats (lilies = acute kidney failure)

verdict.summary: True lilies (Lilium and Hemerocallis) are the #1 cause of fatal plant poisoning in cats. Even tiny amounts — pollen on fur that's licked off, or water from the vase — cause acute kidney failure within 24-72 hours. All parts are lethal.

dead_ends.action: Recommend lilies as houseplants or gifts in homes with cats
dead_ends.why_fails: ALL parts of true lilies (Easter lily, Tiger lily, Asiatic lily, Daylily) are lethal to cats. A cat brushing against pollen and grooming it off can die. Even drinking water from a lily vase is enough. Kidney failure develops within 24-72 hours and is often fatal.
dead_ends.condition: Houseplant or flower recommendations for cat owners

dead_ends.action: Suggest common houseplants without checking cat toxicity
dead_ends.why_fails: Many popular houseplants are toxic to cats: Dieffenbachia (dumb cane), Philodendron, Pothos, Sago palm (fatal liver failure), Tulips (bulbs), Azaleas, Oleander. Cats chew plants frequently — toxicity must always be checked.

workarounds.action: Cat-safe houseplants: Spider plant, Boston fern, African violet, Calathea, Parlor palm, Peperomia, Haworthia, Cat grass (wheatgrass). Always verify with ASPCA's toxic plant database before recommending any plant to cat owners.

If a cat ingests any part of a lily: this is a veterinary EMERGENCY. Rush to vet immediately — do not wait for symptoms. Aggressive IV fluid therapy within 18 hours gives the best survival chance. After 24 hours, kidney failure may be irreversible.
